authoritative_sources.title: Click to Cancel: The FTC's amended Negative Option Rule and what it means for your business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC guidance defining material-term disclosure, proof of consent, and simple cancellation expectations for recurring memberships sold online, by phone, or in person.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from inventing hard-to-exit renewal paths or treating in-person enrollments as if remote cancellation were optional.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance defining billing-error handling, merchant-versus-issuer boundaries, and timing expectations relevant to duplicate charges, missing refunds, and disputed membership fees.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from promising issuer outcomes or resolving disputes solely from local notes when the payment path requires billing-error escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ accessibility guidance for venue and attraction ticket sales, including accessible seating, companion seats, price parity, subscription or season-ticket handling, and third-party sales.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accessible seating, companion ticket, and box-office handoff handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence-backed routing when accessible seating inventory, release, or comparable-seat questions depend on specialized box-office or accommodations authority.

authoritative_sources.title: ASTC Passport Program FAQ
authoritative_sources.publisher: Association of Science and Technology Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ASTC reciprocal-admission guidance for science centers and museums covering named cardholder rules, local-area exclusions, group limits, special-exhibit exclusions, and forgotten-card handling.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating reciprocal admission as universal or transferable when local-area limits, named-cardholder checks, or special-exhibit exclusions materially change the answer.

authoritative_sources.title: AZA Reciprocal Admissions Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AZA reciprocity guidance for zoos and aquariums covering proof-of-membership requirements, discount outcomes, family-count differences, special-event exclusions, and close-geographic-proximity restrictions.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds concrete refusal triggers when a guest wants a borrowed pass honored or expects nearby institutions to ignore reciprocal-admission limits.

authoritative_sources.title: Timed Entry Permits - Questions & Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NPS operating guidance for pass-plus-reservation access covering separate timed-entry permit requirements, non-transferability, ID checks, non-refundable processing fees, anti-counterfeit handling, and one-day validity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Timed-entry reservation, gate denial, and access-control handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from treating passes as sufficient when a separate reservation, named holder, QR confirmation, or fee policy still governs entry.

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC fraud-control guidance for suspicious account activity and identity-risk indicators relevant to wallet-pass reissue, email changes, replacement membership cards, and refund rerouting.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating suspicious membership changes as ordinary profile edits when identity-theft controls may apply.

authoritative_sources.title: Cal/OSHA Amusement Ride and Tramway Unit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state safety-regulator guidance defining amusement-ride and tramway inspection authority, insurance and permitting controls, and the separation between member support and safety oversight.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ride or attraction safety boundary recognition
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from giving safety assurances or closing ride-related complaints that belong to regulated operations and safety owners.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and member-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the member, passholder, household, venue, event, visit date, reservation window, and requested outcome before acting.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Entitlement, reservation, and reciprocity verification
workflow_stages.description: Review membership terms, renewal state, tier benefits, blackout dates, reciprocal-participation limits, timed-entry permits, and named-holder requirements before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Payment, promotion, and access-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Compare CRM, ticketing, POS, payment, refund, promotion, and gate-scan records to establish the documented workflow state.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Supported member-experience resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using documented membership, reservation, reciprocal-admission, and communication rules without promising unsupported exceptions or access outcomes.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Accessibility, fraud, safety, repeat-failure, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported exceptions, accessible-seating ownership questions, suspicious account changes, ride or facility safety concerns, repeat failures, and public-source or tenant-policy conflicts for the correct next owner.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

artifacts: membership agreements, published membership terms, renewal authorizations, and tier-benefit matrices
CRM member profiles, household rosters, digital pass or membership-card records, and communication logs
timed-entry reservations, ticketing records, gate or turnstile scan logs, and reservation-confirmation artifacts
promotion records, guest-allotment limits, reciprocal-admission rules, and special-exhibit or event exclusions
POS transactions, payment-tender history, refund records, processor notes, and duplicate-charge or chargeback indicators
accessible seating notes, companion-ticket instructions, incident tickets, fraud flags, manager approvals, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: POS and payment processor or merchant portal

allowed: Classify membership-experience work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented member, reservation, scan, payment, and policy artifacts.

Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, communication summaries, escalation packages, and program or membership status summaries without claiming pricing, accessibility, safety, or fraud final authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance, venue rules, and tenant policy that materially change the allowed cancellation path, accessible-seating answer, reciprocity outcome, refund posture, or transferability answer.
Requests requiring pricing or refund approval, seat-inventory control, accessible-seating determination, fraud adjudication, ride or facility safety review, or incident signoff.
Repeated member, scan, reservation, or access failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Backdate cancellations, waive blackout dates, exceed guest allotments, transfer access credentials, or grant member-only pricing exceptions without documented authority.
Advise that a ride, venue, exhibit, or security condition is safe, cleared, or ready for guest use.
Treat arts, entertainment, and recreation membership work as generic subscription support that ignores reciprocal-admission, timed-entry, accessible-seating, gate-scan, or venue-policy evidence.

domain_failure_modes: membership work proceeds without the member, venue, reservation, or named-cardholder identifiers that materially change the answer
box-office or accessibility inventory decisions are handled as ordinary membership updates
reciprocal-admission benefits are treated as universal even when local-area, event, or special-exhibit exclusions apply
passes or timed-entry reservations are treated as transferable despite named-holder, ID, or anti-counterfeit controls
ride or facility complaints receive unsupported safety reassurance instead of regulated operations escalation
fraud-sensitive account or refund changes are processed as routine profile maintenance
public cancellation or payment-dispute guidance conflicts with tenant scripts but the lane still gives a confident answer
repeat gate-scan and reservation failures are recycled through generic member-service responses without preserving blocker history

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Scenario proving high-volume intake or service execution
Scenario proving customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when pricing, refund, or safety policy cannot be overridden
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when customer harm, fraud, or policy exceptions determine the answer
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Reciprocal-admission participation, blackout dates, guest counts, companion-ticket rules, and special-event exclusions vary widely by institution and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack remains validated because those differences are turned into explicit tenant and venue retrieval requirements. The specialist escalates whenever facility-specific reciprocity or access rules are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Safety ownership differs across amusement rides, animal interactions, live-event incidents, and local facility emergency plans.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane does not attempt to normalize safety signoff. Runtime must retrieve the facility-specific operations and safety owner map before any case with safety implications can be advanced.

notes: The research baseline intentionally combines federal consumer-protection guidance, public accessibility rules, recognized museum and zoo reciprocity references, public recreation reservation guidance, and fraud and safety controls so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because venue-specific reciprocity, seating, reservation, and incident differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at membership administration and evidence-backed communication. Pricing overrides, seat-inventory control, accessible-seating decisions, fraud adjudication, and ride or facility safety signoff remain outside the lane.
